key_insight: Large language models can help automate cancer registry abstraction, with the best models performing well on simpler registry variables and more moderately on complex date extraction.
clinical_relevance: If validated and integrated carefully, LLMs could reduce manual chart-review burden for cancer registries and speed up population-level cancer surveillance.
method: Evaluated five foundation LLMs using zero-shot prompting
Abstracted eight registry variables from clinical reports
Tested on 5,939 patients across seven cancer types
evidence_signal: In a real-world hospital registry benchmark, top models reached about 0.8 F1 for low-cardinality variables and about 0.55 exact accuracy for diagnosis and treatment dates, improving to about 0.85 within a ±30-day window.
watch_item: Performance was lower for precise date extraction, so human review and workflow validation remain important before routine use.
paper_title: Large language models for cancer registry abstraction: a real-world evaluation across models, variables, and cancer types
